title: Maximum Number of Occurrences of a Substring
notes: Time complexity scales roughly linearly as n increases by a factor of 10. The time complexity is bounded by O(N * minSize) and since minSize <= 26, it reduces to O(N). Space complexity is theoretically O(N) or bounded by the maximum number of unique strings of length minSize (which is a constant at most 26^minSize), as evidenced by the flat/decreasing memory footprint.
